checks.description: Primary CTA is visible and specific above the fold.
Phone, form, map, or quote path is clear.
Visitor can identify business category and service area quickly.
Copy says what happens after contact or quote request.
Services match the niche and are not generic filler.
Names concrete customer problems, objections, or urgency.
Uses city, service area, climate, property type, or local intent when known.
Preserved facts or audit observations influence the copy.
Includes proof mechanism: reviews, process, photos, certifications, warranties, project examples, or safe placeholders.
Does not present AI-generated or unverified proof as real.
Addresses common risks such as no-obligation quote, inspection clarity, cleanup, timelines, or transparent pricing.
Google/customer reviews are used when available; AI reference reviews are clearly replaceable.
Core services are covered without overloading the page.
Hero, services, proof/about, process/FAQ, and final CTA support conversion.
Offer fits the lead type: teaser preview, redesign, one-page, or multi-page.
Contact form asks only useful fields for the niche and page type.
Title/H1 include business type and location when known.
Name, address, phone, hours, and service area can support NAP consistency.
Copy provides fields needed for LocalBusiness schema.
FAQ includes real local-service questions, not generic marketing fluff.
Sounds like a real local business, not corporate filler.
Headings, bullets, labels, and short paragraphs are easy to scan.
Tone matches niche and template style.
No obvious grammar, spelling, or awkward translation issues.
Gives a concrete reason to choose the business.
For redesigns, copy explains what the new site fixes or improves.
Copy produces a credible cold outreach angle and mockup rationale.
Contact details match the lead profile/evidence.
Unsupported claims are absent or marked as placeholders.
No internal workflow terms leak into customer-facing copy.

grades.meaning: Strong enough for approval; only polish if needed.
Good structure; targeted improvements should lift it.
Major copy problems; likely weak conversion.
Unsafe, generic, or misleading; rebuild the brief.
